perturbation.original_text: For the avoidance of doubt, the terms "Total Adjusted Capital" and "Company Action Level RBC" shall have the meanings ascribed thereto under the insurance laws and regulations of the Domiciliary State, or, if not defined therein, shall have the meanings ascribed thereto in the risk-based capital ("RBC") instructions promulgated by the National Association of Insurance Commissioners ("NAIC").
perturbation.changed_text: For the avoidance of doubt, the terms "Total Adjusted Capital" and "Company Action Level RBC" shall have the meanings as solely determined by AIG, irrespective of the definitions under the insurance laws and regulations of the Domiciliary State or the risk-based capital ("RBC") instructions promulgated by the National Association of Insurance Commissioners ("NAIC").
perturbation.explanation: The original text defers to regulatory definitions for key financial terms. The modified text allows AIG to define these terms unilaterally, creating a contradiction. This shift introduces uncertainty about how capital adequacy will be assessed, potentially undermining regulatory oversight.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Texas Insurance Code § 36.002 - Standards for determination of financial condition
perturbation.law_explanation: Texas Insurance Code § 36.002 provides the standards for determining the financial condition of an insurer, including considering assets, liabilities, and required capital. Allowing AIG to unilaterally define "Total Adjusted Capital" and "Company Action Level RBC" directly contradicts the requirement that the financial condition be determined according to the state's insurance laws.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: ï»¿ INSURANCE CODE CHAPTER 36. DEPARTMENT RULES AND PROCEDURES INSURANCE CODE TITLE 2. TEXAS DEPARTMENT OF INSURANCE SUBTITLE A. ADMINISTRATION OF THE TEXAS DEPARTMENT OF INSURANCE CHAPTER 36. DEPARTMENT RULES AND PROCEDURES SUBCHAPTER A. RULES Sec. 36.001. GENERAL RULEMAKING AUTHORITY. (a) The commissioner may adopt any rules necessary and appropriate to implement the powers and duties of the department under this code and other laws of this state. (b) Rules adopted under this section must have general and uniform application. Added by Acts 1999, 76th Leg., ch. 101, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1999. Amended by Acts 2003, 78th Leg., ch. 206, Sec. 15.01, eff. June 11, 2003. Sec. 36.002. ADDITIONAL RULEMAKING AUTHORITY. The commissioner may adopt reasonable rules that are: (1) necessary to effect the purposes of a provision of: (A) Subchapter B, Chapter 5; (B) Subchapter C , Chapter 1806 ; (C) Subchapter A , Chapter 2301 ; (D) Chapter 251 , as that chapter relates to casualty insurance and fidelity, guaranty, and surety bond insurance; (E) Chapter 253 ; (F) Chapter 2008 , 2251 , or 2252 ; or (G) Subtitle B, Title 10; or (2) appropriate to accomplish the purposes of a provision of: (A) Section 37.051 (a), 403.002 , 501.159 , 941.003 (b)(1) or (c), or 942.003 (b)(1) or (c); (B) Subchapter H , Chapter 544 ; (C) Chapter 251 , as that chapter relates to: (i) automobile insurance; (ii) casualty insurance and fidelity, guaranty, and surety bond insurance; (iii) fire insurance and allied lines; (iv) workers' compensation insurance; or (v) aircraft insurance; (D) Chapter 5, 252 , 253 , 254 , 255 , 256 , 426 , 493 , 494 , 1804 , 1805 , 1806 , 2171 , 6001 , 6002 , or 6003 ; (E) Subtitle B, C, D, E, F, H, or I, Title 10; (F) Section 417.008 , Government Code; or (G) Chapter 2154 , Occupations Code. Added by Acts 2005, 79th Leg., Ch. 727 (H.B. 2017 ), Sec. 11, eff. April 1, 2007. Amended by: Acts 2007, 80th Leg., R.S., Ch. 730 (H.B. 2636 ), Sec. 2B.002, eff. April 1, 2009. Acts 2007, 80th Leg., R.S., Ch. 730 (H.B. 2636 ), Sec. 3B.0591(d), eff. September 1, 2007. Acts 2007, 80th Leg., R.S., Ch. 921 (H.B. 3167 ), Sec. 9.0591(d), eff. September 1, 2007. Acts 2017, 85th Leg., R.S., Ch. 594 (S.B. 1070 ), Sec. 3.01, eff. September 1, 2017. Sec. 36.003. RULES RESTRICTING ADVERTISING OR COMPETITIVE BIDDING. The commissioner may not adopt rules restricting advertising or competitive bidding by a person regulated by the department except to prohibit false, misleading, or deceptive practices by the person. Added by Acts 1999, 76th Leg., ch. 101, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1999. Sec. 36.004. COMPLIANCE WITH NATIONAL ASSOCIATION OF INSURANCE COMMISSIONERS REQUIREMENTS; RULES. (
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows AIG to unilaterally define key financial terms, contradicting the intention of regulatory oversight as described in the perturbation explanation. The original contract defers to regulatory definitions, while the modified text allows AIG to define the terms independently. Although Scraped Law Snippet 1 doesn't directly state the specifics of financial condition determination, it emphasizes the commissioner's authority to adopt rules to implement the powers and duties of the department under this code, reinforcing the idea that financial terms should be governed by regulatory standards, not individual entities like AIG. The misalignment between regulatory definitions and AIG's definitions would create a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All contributions contemplated under this Agreement shall be approved and made in compliance with applicable law, including, without limitation, approval by the board of directors of each applicable entity and any prior notice or approval requirements specified under applicable rules and regulations.
perturbation.changed_text: All contributions contemplated under this Agreement shall be made at AIG's discretion, without necessarily requiring approval and without strict adherence to applicable law, unless explicitly required by AIG's internal policies.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates compliance with 'applicable law'. The revised text suggests that AIG's internal policies supersede legal requirements, creating a contradiction. This introduces a compliance risk, as contributions might not meet legal standards.
perturbation.law_explanation: Texas Insurance Code § 82.001 et seq. regulates the investments of insurance companies, including capital contributions. The modified text allows for contributions without necessarily complying with these investment regulations, contradicting the legal requirements for insurer solvency and financial stability.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: ï»¿ INSURANCE CODE CHAPTER 82. SANCTIONS INSURANCE CODE TITLE 2. TEXAS DEPARTMENT OF INSURANCE SUBTITLE B. DISCIPLINE AND ENFORCEMENT CHAPTER 82. SANCTIONS SUBCHAPTER A. GENERAL PROVISIONS Sec. 82.001. DEFINITION. In this chapter, "authorization" means a permit, license, certificate of authority, certificate of registration, or other authorization issued or existing under the commissioner's authority or this code. Added by Acts 1999, 76th Leg., ch. 101, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1999. Sec. 82.002. APPLICATION OF CHAPTER. (a) This chapter applies to each company regulated by the commissioner, including: (1) a domestic or foreign, stock or mutual, life, health, or accident insurance company; (2) a domestic or foreign, stock or mutual, fire or casualty insurance company; (3) a Mexican casualty company; (4) a domestic or foreign Lloyd's plan insurer; (5) a domestic or foreign reciprocal or interinsurance exchange; (6) a domestic or foreign fraternal benefit society; (7) a domestic or foreign title insurance company; (8) a stipulated premium insurance company; (9) a nonprofit legal service corporation; (10) a health maintenance organization; (11) a statewide mutual assessment company; (12) a local mutual aid association; (13) a local mutual burial association; (14) an association exempt under Section 887.102 ; (15) a nonprofit hospital, medical, or dental service corporation, including a company subject to Chapter 842 ; (16) a county mutual insurance company; and (17) a farm mutual insurance company. (b) This chapter also applies to: (1) an agent of an entity described by Subsection (a); and (2) an individual or a corporation, association, partnership, or other artificial person who: (A) is engaged in the business of insurance; (B) holds an authorization; or (C) is regulated by the commissioner. (c) The commissioner's authority under this chapter applies to each form of authorization and each person or entity holding an authorization. Added by Acts 1999, 76th Leg., ch. 101, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1999. Amended by Acts 2001, 77th Leg., ch. 1419, Sec. 18, eff. June 1, 2003; Acts 2001, 77th Leg., ch. 1420, Sec. 11.002, eff. Sept. 1, 2001. Amended by: Acts 2007, 80th Leg., R.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states contributions will be made 'without strict adherence to applicable law' unless required by AIG's internal policies. This contradicts the original text, which mandates compliance with 'applicable law'. The Law Explanation suggests this may violate Texas Insurance Code § 82.001 et seq., which regulates insurance company investments, including capital contributions to ensure financial stability. Scraped Law Snippet 1, particularly Sec. 82.002, clarifies the broad applicability of Chapter 82, covering various insurance companies and related entities regulated by the commissioner. The altered text's explicit disregard for 'applicable law' directly undermines the regulatory framework established by the Insurance Code, thus creating a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Any policyholder holding a policy issued by the Company prior to the termination of this Agreement shall have the right to demand that the Company enforce the Company's rights under paragraphs 1, 3 and 4 of this Agreement, and, if the Company fails or refuses to take timely action to enforce such rights or the Company defaults in any claim or other payment owed to any such policyholder when due, such policyholder may proceed directly against AIG to enforce the Company's rights under paragraphs 1, 3 and 4 of this Agreement
perturbation.changed_text: Any policyholder holding a policy issued by the Company shall only have recourse against the Company, and under no circumstance shall the policyholder be allowed to proceed directly against AIG to enforce the Company's rights under paragraphs 1, 3, and 4 of this Agreement. AIG's obligations are solely to the Company, and not to any third-party policyholder.
perturbation.explanation: The original text provides policyholders with a right to proceed directly against AIG under certain circumstances. The modified text removes this right entirely, stating that AIG has no obligations to policyholders. This creates a direct conflict.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Texas Insurance Code § 21.21 - Unfair Methods of Competition and Unfair or Deceptive Acts or Practices Defined
perturbation.law_explanation: Texas Insurance Code § 21.21 prohibits unfair or deceptive acts or practices in the business of insurance. Removing the policyholder's right to proceed against AIG, when the agreement was in place and impacted the solvency of the insurance company, could be seen as diminishing benefits under the policy and an unfair practice if AIG fails to adequately maintain the Company's capital, which undermines the policyholder's reasonable expectations and therefore may be considered to violate the mentioned statute.
perturbation.justification: The modified text directly contradicts the original agreement by removing the policyholder's right to proceed directly against AIG. The original text explicitly allows policyholders to take action against AIG under certain conditions, but the changed text eliminates this right entirely, stating that policyholders have no recourse against AIG. This is a clear and direct contradiction, even without supporting scraped snippets, as it reverses a previously granted legal right, resulting in a condition that would likely fail legal scrutiny.
